* Barbell Hang Power Snatch (HPS) x 4
* Overhead Squat (OHS) x2

Barbell Loads

Sc1: 30/35 kg
Sc2: 25/35 kg

title: WOD

sections.content:
* Huggers
* Hip Openers
* ATG Split Squat w OH RB (left & right)
* Scap Row Circles
* Spiderman Lunge
* Glute Bridge Pulses
* ATG Tibialis Raise
* ATG KoT Calf Raise Unilateral (left & right)
This is simply a warm-up for the WOD. Try to end with a heavier set than you will use in the WOD, then strip the weights off to start the WOD.

* GHD Hip Extension DB Row (2 x 15/22.5kg)
* Box Jump
* Wallball Shots
* Airbike

Write scores separately for each movement then add them all together and put the total into the reps box.
Write WB, Barbell & DB weights in Notes
Record scaling options: drop 1 scaling level for every exercise which was not completed at Rx level

Cossack Lunges

Scap Row Circles
Rings Top Support - Neutral Grip
Rings Top Support - Rings Turned Out (RTO)
Ring Flyes (protracted shoulders)
Reverse Flyes
Hanging Knee Raises

Hanging L-Sit Hold to Inverted Hang
Inverted Hang to L-Sit Hold
Inverted Hand with turned-out hands
Scap Pull-Ups
Ring Chin-Ups (think of pulling the rings down rather than pulling yourself up
Ring Chin-Ups with 2 secs hold in the middle
Unilateral Rows
Face Pulls: Rings set below the hips, 4 steps back (2 steps back is more difficult), feet at shoulder width,pronated grip, legs fully extended, scapular retraction, pull the rings back to make a goalpost shape, elbows at 90° out to the sides, hold for 1 second, repeat
Prone Rings Biceps Curls
Rings Roll-Out

coach_notes:
Snatch workout - ZT - https://youtu.be/rV1OsGZS_R0?si=kmpNc-SUd4sBevGW

Catalyst Exercise Library - https://www.catalystathletics.com/exercises/

Early Arm Bend in the Snatch & Clean - Catalyst

Womens 75kg 2018 European Weightlifting Championship - Lydia Valentin
Mens 94kg 2018 European Weightlifting Championship

Catalyst Warm-Up - https://youtu.be/XF5LLh7kmfk
Catalyst Dynamic Warm-Up - https://youtu.be/XhHc7FbSUiY

Barbell Snatch Technique Primers:
Dip Muscle Snatch - Catalyst - https://www.catalystathletics.com/exercise/464/Dip-Muscle-Snatch/
Muscle Snatch from Power Position - Monroe Miller - https://www.facebook.com/reel/543227008885980

Muscle Snatch from Power Position - https://www.catalystathletics.com/exercise/466/Muscle-Snatch-From-Power-Position/

Execution:
Start standing tall with the bar hanging at arms’ length in your snatch grip. 
Ensure even whole foot balance and brace your trunk. 
Bend at the knees only with a vertical trunk just as you would for a jerk, actively pushing the bar against your hips, and stop.
After a 1-2 second pause, drive up with the legs and complete a muscle snatch.
Focus on keeping the bar as close as possible, finishing with strong vertical leg drive, and punching vertically into the overhead position at the end of the turnover.
It can be helpful to think of the movement as a snatch high-pull with an added turnover of the bar afterward. This will reinforce the idea of driving vertically and lifting the elbows high and out before the turnover.
The only difference between a dip muscle snatch and a muscle snatch from power position is that there is a pause in the bottom of the dip in the muscle snatch from power position. 

Purpose
The muscle snatch is helpful to learn, reinforce and strengthen the proper upper body mechanics of the turnover (third pull) of the snatch. The power position variation helps focus on vertical leg drive and better bar-body interaction to improve bar proximity. The abbreviated motion can also be used to reduce the contribution of the legs and place more emphasis on upper body strength. From power position relative to dip will allow more focus on proper position, balance and leg drive.

Rep scheme:
6 x Muscle Snatch from Power Position with PVC Pipe on coach's cue
6 x Muscle Snatch from Power position with Empty Bar on coach's cue
6 x Dip Muscle Snatch with Empty Bar on coach's cue
Add weight for sets of 3-4

Hang Power Snatch Practice (6 mins)
1 minute Snatch Tutorial
Work up to WOD weight for Hang Power Snatch
sets of 2 x HPS + 1 OHS

WOD 12 min AMRAP
2 x Chest Touch Bear Crawl over the Bar (1 x fwds, 1 x bwds. Start in Bear Crawl position, touch chest to ground, BC over the bar, touch chest to ground, reverse BC over the bar, touch chest to ground)
4 x Hang Power Snatch
2 x Barbell Overhead Squats

* Push-up Strict
* Minute 1: 5 secs down, 5 secs up x 6 reps: 60 seconds
* Minute 2: REST
* Minute 3: (weighted) (same format as above with plate on the back)
Write down scaling option and weight used if any.

* Pull-Up Strict
* Minute 1: 5 secs up, 5 secs down x 6 reps: 60 seconds
* Minute 2: REST
* Minute 3:(weighted) same format as above with plate hanging from belt

* Parallettes L-Sit Hold
Minute 1: 20 secs unbroken hold
Minute 2: REST
Minute 3: 20 secs unbroken hold

* KB OH Carry
Minute 1: 60 secs for metres, light weight
Minute 2: REST
Minute 3: 60 secs for metres, heavy (WOD) weight
NOTE: Record the 2nd set

WOD Explanation:
2 rounds of 6 movements:
1 min per movement, 15 seconds change over
2 mins REST between rounds
* Push-up Strict
* Pull-Up Strict
* Parallettes L-Sit Hold

* KB OH Carry
2 x KB held overhead
* C2 Skierg
* C2 Rower
Put your total score here

* Scorpion
* Frog Stretch with Reverse Hands
* Couch

coach_notes:
Focus today is on strict precise movement, no kipping, jerking or swinging
If any exercise is too easy, we add weight, if too hard, we scale it to make it easier
Class is planned for 1 hour plus stretching which athletes can do alone

* Push-ups Strict (6 mins)
PoP:
Straight line from shoulders to heels, no raising or sagging in the hips
Head/neck in neutral position, do not lower or raise the head
Feet together, touching
Hands underneath and slightly outside the shoulders
Elbows go backwards diagonally, not out to the sides
On Timer & Coach's cue
Strict Push-Ups
Each rep should last 10 seconds, 5 seconds down, 5 seconds up
Min 1: 6 x Push-Ups in 60 secs
Min 2: REST
Min 3: 6 x Weighted Push-Ups in 60 secs
Scale up or down as needed but try and work for the full 60 seconds

Push-Ups scale up: add weight plate on upper back
Choose weight to make it as difficult as possible but make sure you can complete the 6 reps within 60 secs

Push-Ups Scale down:
Sc1: Strict negative, knees down, push-up, raise knees to start position
Sc2: Begin on knees and perform as above
If 6 reps cannot be completed with good form on Sc2, reduce the reps to 5 or 4 accordingly

* Pull-Ups Strict chin over bar (6 mins)
Same format as above where we add weight to make it more difficult or scale it to make it easier
Min 1: 6 x Strict Pull-Ups in 60 seconds (IGYG with partner)
Min 2: REST
Min 3: 6 x Strict Weighted Pull-Ups in 60 seconds (IGYG with partner)
Pull-Ups scale up: add weight plate to weight belt, 
Choose weight to make it as difficult as possible but make sure you can complete the 6 reps within 60 secs

Pull-Ups Scale down:
Banded Pull-Ups - every rep starts from a static position, no bouncing, swinging or kipping

* Parallettes L-Sit Hold (6 mins)
2 rounds of:
Min 1: 10-20 secs unbroken Parallettes L-Sit Hold in 60 secs
Min 2: REST
Min 3: 10-20 secs unbroken Parallettes L-Sit Hold in 60 secs
Scale down as needed but aim for a shorter time (min 10 seconds) with a more difficult scale, rather than a longer time with an easier scale

L-Sits Scale (as in homework):
Rx: Straight legs, feet and legs together, feet @ 90° to hips or higher, shoulders behind wrists
Sc1: Bent knees or straight legs @ < 90°
Sc2: Bent knees in tuck position, shoulders in front of wrists

* KB OH Carry 2 x KB held overhead (6 mins)
2 rounds of:
Min 1: 60 secs unbroken KB OH Carry for metres & weight (light weight)
Min 2: REST
Min 3: 60 secs unbroken KB OH Carry for metres & weight (heavier weight to decide WOD weight)
KB OH Carry
Rx: 2 x ♀ 16kg, ♂ 24kg
Sc1: 2 x ♀ 12kg, ♂ 20kg
Sc2: 2 x ♀ 8kg, ♂ 16kg

WOD: 2 x EMOM 20 mins total (60 secs Work + 15 secs change over, add scores together for each movement for an overall total)
2 rounds of:
Min 1: Max Strict Push-Ups
Min 2: Max Strict Pull-Ups
Min 3: Max secs Parallettes L-Sit Hold
Min 4: Max SkiErg Metres
Min 5: Max metres KB OH Carry
Min 6: Max Rower Metres
Min 7 & 8: REST
NOTES:
These reps/seconds/metres do NOT have to be unbroken
Keep movements consistent with what we practiced during the class
Split large groups up.
Rx/advanced do as written, Scaled do Push-Ups, Pull-Ups, KBOHC, Rower, L-Sit, SkiErg.

* Snatch Drill: Muscle Snatch from Power Position
* Snatch Drill: Dip Muscle Snatch
Start with PVC Pipe x 6 of each, then empty barbell
Continue with sets of 3 Dip Snatch adding weight every round

* Barbell Hang Power Snatch (HPS)
* Overhead Squat (OHS)

Perform sets of 2 x HPS followed by 1 x OHS, add weight up to WOD weight

12 min AMRAP of:
* Chest to ground Bear Crawl over the barbell x 2
* Barbell Hang Power Snatch (HPS) x 4
* Overhead Squat (OHS) x2
Chest touches the ground before the barbell, bear crawl over the bar, touch chest to ground, crawl backwards over the bar, touch chest to ground. This is 1 rep.
Barbell Loads
Rx: 35/45 kg
Sc1: 30/35 kg
Sc2: 25/35 kg

* FR Upper back
* BR QL Release
* BR TFL
* Frog Stretch with Reverse Hands
